Obama then moved to Chicago, Illinois, and began working as a community organizer, helping low-income residents improve their public housing conditions. In 1988, he enrolled at Harvard Law School. During the 1990-1991 academic year, he was president of the prestigious Harvard Law Review, the first African American elected to the position. [truncated]
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Inauguration

events.title: Executive order to close Guantánamo Bay
events.description: Obama signs an executive order to close the U.S. military prison at Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, within one year. Guantánamo Bay is a detention center where individuals considered to be a national threat to the United States are detained and questioned in controversial conditions. His executive order encountered strong Congressional resistance, and the prison remained open after he left office.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Executive order to close Guantánamo Bay

events.title: Signing of first bill
events.description: The Lilly Ledbetter Fair Pay Act become law. The bill makes it easier for people to challenge unequal pay complaints and is designed to help address the wage gap between men and women.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Signing of first bill

events.title: Cap on top executive pay
events.description: Obama forces companies receiving federal bailout money from the federal government to set a cap on executive pay at $500,000.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Cap on top executive pay

events.title: American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009
events.description: The American Recovery and Reinvestment Act is signed into law by President Obama while in Denver, Colorado. The purpose of the act was to save and create jobs and to provide relief to those most affected by the economic crisis of 2008-2009.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009

events.title: Stem cell research
events.description: The president overturns a rule that restricted the amount of federal money that could be used on embryonic stem cell research.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Stem cell research

events.title: New age Fireside Chats
events.description: Obama hosts the first-ever online discussion at the White House. He livestreams himself answering questions asked by people online via the administration’s website.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: New age Fireside Chats

events.title: Justice Sotomayor
events.description: Obama nominates federal judge Sonia Sotomayor to take over for retiring Justice David Souter. She is confirmed in August.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Justice Sotomayor

events.title: Speech in Cairo
events.description: The president gives a speech at Cairo University in Egypt, discussing the U.S. relations with countries in the Middle East.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Speech in Cairo

events.title: Nobel Peace Prize
events.description: Obama is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for “his extraordinary efforts to strengthen international diplomacy and cooperation between peoples.”
events.impact: Recognized contributions to world peace

events.title: HIV travel ban
events.description: Obama lifts a 22-year-old ban that restricts those with HIV/AIDS from entering the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: HIV travel ban

events.title: Hate Crimes Prevention Act
events.description: The Matthew Shepard and James Byrd, Jr., Hate Crimes Prevention Act becomes law to help jurisdictions to investigate and prosecute hate crimes more effectively.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Hate Crimes Prevention Act

events.title: Haiti aid
events.description: Obama pledges $100,000,000 to assist Haiti in recovery from the devastating earthquake that hit the country on January 12.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Haiti aid

events.title: Volcker Rule
events.description: Obama announces the Volcker Rule, which seeks to restrict U.S. banks from engaging in certain speculative investments that would not be in the best interest of the customer.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Volcker Rule

events.title: Nuclear reactor loans
events.description: Obama declares more than $8.3 billion in loan guarantees to Southern Company to construct two new nuclear reactors in Burke County, Georgia.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Nuclear reactor loans

events.title: School grants
events.description: Obama announces $900 million in grants that will be given to under-performing schools if they accept new reforms.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: School grants

events.title: Affordable Care Act
events.description: The Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act, known as Obamacare, becomes law. This is the most significant regulatory makeover of the U.S. healthcare system since the 1960s. Under the new regulations, Obama sought to increase quality, affordability, and access to healthcare.
events.impact: Expanded healthcare access to millions

events.title: East Coast drilling
events.description: Obama announces he will approve exploration for oil and gas in the Gulf of Mexico and off the coast of Virginia, which had been previously banned.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: East Coast drilling

events.title: Reduction of nuclear arms
events.description: President Obama and President Dmitry Medvedev of Russia sign the Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty (START). This is an agreement to reduce the stockpile of nuclear weapons by the United States and Russia.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Reduction of nuclear arms

events.title: Budget funding for NASA
events.description: Obama proposes adding $6 billion to NASA’s budget over the next five years. The funding would be used for space exploration rather than lunar exploration.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Budget funding for NASA

events.title: Deepwater Horizon oil spill
events.description: An explosion on an oil rig off the coast of Louisiana results in 11 deaths and a massive oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico. The oil rig was leased by BP.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Deepwater Horizon oil spill

events.title: West Virginia coal miners
events.description: Obama delivers a eulogy for the 29 West Virginia coal miners who died in an explosion earlier in the month.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: West Virginia coal miners

events.title: Justice Kagan
events.description: Obama nominates Solicitor General Elena Kagan to the U.S. Supreme Court. She is sworn into office in August.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Justice Kagan

events.title: BP oil spill
events.description: Obama discusses the massive oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico caused by BP. The president says that BP will have to pay heavy reparations for their malpractice.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: BP oil spill

events.title: Wall Street reform
events.description: The Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act is signed by President Obama. The Act gives regulators stronger authority when it comes to breaking up large companies and attempts to protect the consumer by restricting practices in consumer loans and credit cards.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Wall Street reform

events.title: Veteran education reform
events.description: Obama puts into practice the post-911 GI Bill. This bill assists in obtaining a free or cheaper college tuition for those who have served in the U.S
events.impact: Provided education and housing for veterans

events.title: Rosa’s law
events.description: Obama signs Rosa’s Law, which changes the diction of federal statutes that use “mental retardation” to “intellectual disability.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Rosa’s law

events.title: Equal access, opportunity, and respect
events.description: The 21 st Century Communications and Video Accessibility Act becomes law. It sets new standards so that Americans who are deaf, blind, or live with a visual impairment have equal access and opportunity to new technological innovations.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Equal access, opportunity, and respect

events.title: Midterm elections
events.description: In the midterm elections, Republicans gained 6 seats in the Senate, reducing the Democrats' majority in that chamber from 18 (59 to 41) to 6 (53 to 47). The GOP also added 63 seats in the House of Representatives, enough to gain control of the House by a 242 to 193 majority in the 112th Congress.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Midterm elections

events.title: Two-year pay freeze proposal
events.description: In attempt to get the deficit under control, the president freezes pay raises on federal employees for two years starting on January 1, 2011.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Two-year pay freeze proposal

events.title: First presidential pardons
events.description: President Obama pardons nine individuals with crimes ranging from drug charges to conspiracy.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: First presidential pardons

events.title: Healthy kids
events.description: Obama signs the Healthy, Hunger-Free Kids Act of 2010. This funds nutrition and free lunch programs, sets new standards for schools, and allocates money for the implementation of these new provisions.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Healthy kids

events.title: Extension of Bush tax cuts
events.description: The Tax Relief, Unemployment Insurance Reauthorization, and Job Creation Act of 2010 becomes law. This extends the tax cuts instituted by President Bush in the early 2000s and also provides additional provisions to promote economic growth in the form of tax cuts.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Extension of Bush tax cuts

events.title: “Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell”
events.description: Obama repeals the “Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell” policy, which allows people to openly be gay, lesbian, and bisexual while serving in the U.S. Armed Forces.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: “Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell”

events.title: Tucson shootings
events.description: At a constituent meeting in Tucson, Arizona, a gunman open fired, shooting 19 people, killing 6 of them. Representative Gabrielle Giffords was shot in the head; she resigned her seat in the House to focus on her recovery. The President and First Lady traveled to Tucson, and Obama offered his remarks during a memorial service for the victims.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Tucson shootings

events.title: New START treaty
events.description: Obama signs a nuclear arms treaty with Russia, called New START, which seeks to develop our security, relations with Russia, and a world without the fear of nuclear weapons.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: New START treaty

events.title: Military trials for detainees
events.description: Obama orders the end on the ban on military trials for detainees at Guantánamo Bay. This order permits the United States to resume military trials for detainees
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Military trials for detainees

events.title: Osama Bin Laden killed
events.description: Obama announces the death of Osama Bin Laden, who was the leader of Al-Qaeda, the terrorist group that claimed responsibility for the U.S. terrorist attacks on September 11, 2001. (Bin Laden was killed on May 2, 2011, in Pakistan but it was still May 1 in the United States when the president announced his death.)
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Osama Bin Laden killed

events.title: Budget Control Act
events.description: The Budget Control Act becomes law. It raises the federal debt ceiling, causes the United States to avoid default, and creates a bipartisan committee to discuss debt issues.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Budget Control Act

events.title: American Jobs Act
events.description: President Obama makes an address to the joint session of Congress to present the American Jobs Act, which is designed to improve the U.S
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: American Jobs Act

events.title: End of Iraq War
events.description: President Obama announces that U.S. troops will be home from Iraq by December and that the U.S. role in the war is over.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: End of Iraq War

events.title: Iranian sanctions
events.description: Obama signs an executive order that freezes the assets of the Iranian government held within the United States due to Iran engaging in what Obama called, “deceptive practices.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Iranian sanctions

events.title: Against insider trading
events.description: The Stop Trading on Congressional Knowledge Act (STOCK) is signed by President Obama. It seeks to eliminate insider trading and prohibits the use of non-public information for profit in the private sector.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Against insider trading

events.title: Gay marriage
events.description: President Obama announces his support of same-sex marriage.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Gay marriage

events.title: Democratic National Convention
events.description: Obama accepts the Democratic nomination for president and speaks to the convention.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Democratic National Convention

events.title: Reelected
events.description: Obama defeats Republican Mitt Romney to win a second term in office with 332 electoral votes to 206 and 51 percent of the popular vote.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Reelected

events.title: Sandy Hook shooting
events.description: The president addresses the nation after a shooting at the Sandy Hook elementary school in Connecticut results in the death of 20 children and 6 adults. He pledges to fight for gun control legislation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Sandy Hook shooting

events.title: Taxpayer relief
events.description: Obama signs the American Taxpayer Relief Act. This extended tax breaks for the majority of Americans while raising the tax rates for top earners.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Taxpayer relief

events.title: Second Inauguration
events.description: Barack Obama is inaugurated into his second term as president. He was sworn into office in a private ceremony on Sunday, January 20, but the official ceremony took place the next day.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Second Inauguration

events.title: Immigration reform
events.description: Obama gives a speech outlining his proposal for comprehensive immigration reform.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Immigration reform

events.title: Violence against women
events.description: The Violence Against Women Reauthorization Act of 2013 becomes law. This act seeks to provide increased protection for women under the law by improving law enforcement tools to combat the violence, improving services for victims of violent crimes, and improving awareness and violence reduction practices.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Violence against women

events.title: Climate plan
events.description: In a speech at Georgetown University, the president announces the Climate Action Plan, which is designed to lower carbon pollution, prepare for the impending effects of climate change, and to address reversing climate change as a global endeavor.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Climate plan

events.title: “Trayvon Martin could have been me”
events.description: Obama delivers remarks on the murder of Trayvon Martin. He discusses why the African American community was enraged at the acquittal of Trayvon Martin’s killer, George Zimmerman. Obama stated, “Trayvon Martin could have been me 35 years ago.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: “Trayvon Martin could have been me”

events.title: Student loans
events.description: Obama signs into law the Bipartisan Student Loan Certainty Act of 2013. This act outlines how the government should distribute loans to students and other tactics for helping students with crippling debt.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Student loans

events.title: Chemical weapons in Syria
events.description: President Obama gives a speech about the war in Syria and President Bashar al-Assad’s use of chemical weapons against his own people.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Chemical weapons in Syria

events.title: Government shutdown
events.description: A shutdown of the federal government begins after Congress fails to agree on funding legislation. It last 16 days.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Government shutdown

events.title: Climate change and its effects
events.description: Obama signs an executive order in order to prepare the United States for climate change. The order addresses the impacts of climate change and details how the United States will take action to increase readiness against climate change.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Climate change and its effects

events.title: State of the Union
events.description: Obama gives his State of the Union address, focusing on the country’s low unemployment and increasing inequality.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: State of the Union

events.title: Agriculture Act of 2014
events.description: The Federal Agriculture Reform and Risk Management Act of 2014 is signed into law. This act authorizes $956 billion for increased awareness and spending on nutrition and agriculture programs in the United States from 2014 to 2018.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Agriculture Act of 2014

events.title: Minimum wage
events.description: Obama signs an executive order to increase the minimum wage from $7.25 to $10.10 for all workers on federal contracts.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Minimum wage

events.title: Russian sanctions
events.description: Obama calls for economic sanctions against those responsible for the Russian intervention in Crimea and the stealing of assets of the Ukrainian people.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Russian sanctions

events.title: VA Secretary resigns
events.description: Secretary of Veteran’s Affairs, General Eric Shinseki, resigns after it was revealed that veterans had to wait months for care at VA hospitals.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: VA Secretary resigns

events.title: LGBT discrimination
events.description: Obama signs an executive order to protect the rights of LGBT employees in the workforce. This does not include an exemption for religious beliefs, which means people cannot discriminate based on their religious preference.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: LGBT discrimination

events.title: Midterm elections
events.description: Republicans retake control of Congress during the midterm elections.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Midterm elections

events.title: Child Care grant
events.description: The Child Care and Development Block Grant of 2014 is signed into law. This act provides grants to states so they can assist low-income families in finding child care for their children.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Child Care grant

events.title: Immigration reform
events.description: Obama makes speech to the nation on comprehensive immigration reform. He announces he will use his executive power to allow illegal immigrants currently living within the United States to legally stay and obtain work.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Immigration reform

events.title: Free community college
events.description: Obama proposes to make community college free for students if they adhere to certain criteria such as maintaining a certain GPA. He also outlines what community colleges and the federal government must do in order to facilitate this change.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Free community college

events.title: Veteran suicide epidemic
events.description: The Clay Hunt Suicide Prevention for American Veterans Act is signed into law. This act seeks to raise awareness on veteran suicide, increases opportunity for veterans to receive help, and also makes the Pentagon and Veterans Affairs Department submit reviews of their prevention programs.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Veteran suicide epidemic

events.title: 50th anniversary of Selma marches
events.description: President Obama and his family take part in the 50 th anniversary of the civil rights marches in Selma, Alabama.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: 50th anniversary of Selma marches

events.title: Eulogy for Reverend Clementa Pinckney
events.description: Obama delivers a eulogy for Reverend Clementa Pinckney in Charleston, South Carolina, following a racially charged shooting that killed nine people worshipping inside a church.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Eulogy for Reverend Clementa Pinckney

events.title: Gay marriage upheld
events.description: Supreme Court rules 5-4 that the Constitution guarantees a right to same-sex marriage.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Gay marriage upheld

events.title: Clean Power Plan
events.description: The President along with the Environmental Protection Agency introduces the Clean Power Plan to reduce carbon pollution from existing power plants by more than 30 percent by 2030.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Clean Power Plan

events.title: Pope Francis
events.description: Obama welcomes Pope Francis for visit at the White House.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Pope Francis

events.title: Paris Climate Change conference
events.description: President Obama attends in the United Nations Climate Change Conference in Paris, France.
events.impact: Created framework for international cooperation

events.title: Deal with Iran
events.description: Obama announces historic agreement with Iran to prevent the acquisition of nuclear weapons. Iran met conditions for the United States to begin lifting some economic sanctions.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Deal with Iran

events.title: Death of Antonin Scalia
events.description: Supreme Court Justice Antonin Scalia is found dead. Obama nominated Merrick Garland to replace Scalia on the Supreme Court but Republicans in Congress will not hold confirmation hearings.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Death of Antonin Scalia

events.title: Cuba trip
events.description: Obama becomes the first sitting U.S. president to visit Cuba since 1928.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Cuba trip

events.title: Flint, Michigan visit
events.description: Following the discovery that Flint, Michigan’s water supply had been insufficiently treated and had exposed its residents to dangerously high levels of lead, the President traveled to Flint to try to reassure the people.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Flint, Michigan visit

events.title: Supreme Court upholds health care law
events.description: The Supreme Court rules 6-3 that tax credits are available to eligible citizens for insurance purchased on any exchange created under the ACA.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Supreme Court upholds health care law

events.title: Gives speech at Democratic National Convention
events.description: Obama gives a speech at the Democratic National Convention in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, in support of Democratic presidential candidate Hillary Clinton. November 8, 2016: Presidential election Republican candidate Donald J. Trump defeats Hillary Clinton, winning the presidency with 306 electoral votes (although he lost the popular vote by nearly 3 million votes). November 10, 2016: President-elect visits White House President Obama hosts President-elect Donald Trump at the White House, meeting with the press in the Oval Office. January 20, 2017: Leaving office President Obama concludes his last day in office. After attending the inauguration of President Trump, the Obamas head off for a vacation in Palm Springs, California. The Obama announced that they will continue to live in Washington, DC, until their younger daughter finishes high school.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Gives speech at Democratic National Convention

events.title: Trump inaugurated
events.description: Trump is inaugurated as the 45th President of the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump inaugurated

events.title: Women's March
events.description: Millions of people around the world participate in the Women’s March, the largest single-day march in US history, to protest the Trump administration and its policies.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Women's March

events.title: Withdrawal from the Trans-Pacific Partnership
events.description: President Trump directs the office of the US trade representatives to withdraw the United States from the Trans-Pacific Partnership.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Withdrawal from the Trans-Pacific Partnership

events.title: Global gag rule reinstated
events.description: In one of his first policy decisions, President Trump decides to reinstate the “Mexico City Policy,” a ban on aid to international groups performing or counseling on abortion. President Ronald Reagan first introduced this ban in 1984 at a U.N conference in Mexico City, and U.S. presidents have consistently rescinded and reinstated since.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Global gag rule reinstated

events.title: Travel ban
events.description: President Trump signs an executive order that denies entry into the United States for people from Iran, Iraq, Libya, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, and Yemen, and suspends the Refugee Admissions Policy for 120 days. Trump’s travel ban sparks large protests and legal challenges. As a result of legal challenges, Trump revises the executive order.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Travel ban

events.title: Gorsuch nominated for SCOTUS
events.description: President Trump announces his nomination of Neil Gorsuch to the US Supreme Court. Judge Gorsuch will fill the seat vacated when Justice Antonin Scalia died in February 2016. The US Senate denied President Barack Obama the chance to fill the seat.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Gorsuch nominated for SCOTUS

events.title: Flynn resigns
events.description: President Trump’s National Security Advisor Michael T. Flynn resigns after he admits to misleading Vice President Mike Pence about conversations Flynn had with the Russian ambassador before Trump’s inauguration.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Flynn resigns

events.title: New executive order on the travel ban
events.description: President Trump signs a revised travel ban to respond to legal challenges to the first travel ban. The new travel ban implements a travel ban from six Muslim majority countries and temporarily suspends the Refugee Admission Program.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: New executive order on the travel ban

events.title: Reversal on environmental protection
events.description: President Trump signs an executive order that rolls back the Obama administration’s temporary ban on mining coal and a protection rule for streams. Trump reverses much of Obama’s clean power strategy, rolling back US action to combat climate change.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Reversal on environmental protection

events.title: Action against Syrian government
events.description: President Trump orders US strikes against an air base in Syria after the Syrian government launches a chemical weapons attack against the Syrian province of Idlib, killing civilians including children.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Action against Syrian government

events.title: Neil M. Gorsuch joins the US Supreme Court
events.description: Judge Neil M. Gorsuch is sworn into the US Supreme Court, replacing Justice Antonin Scalia who died in 2016.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Neil M. Gorsuch joins the US Supreme Court

events.title: FBI director James Comey removed
events.description: President Donald Trump fires director of the FBI, James Comey. The firing is unexpected and raises concerns about abuse of power and the independence of the FBI.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: FBI director James Comey removed

events.title: Robert Muller chosen as special counsel
events.description: Deputy Attorney General Rod Rosenstein names Robert Muller, former director of the FBI under Presidents George W. Bush and Barack Obama, as special counsel to investigate Russia’s interference in the 2016 election.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Robert Muller chosen as special counsel

events.title: Withdrawal from Paris climate accord
events.description: President Trump announces that the United States will withdraw from the 2015 Paris climate accord, another agreement negotiated by Obama.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Withdrawal from Paris climate accord

events.title: Chief of Staff Kelly named
events.description: President Trump announces that he has replaced Reince Priebus as his chief of staff with John F. Kelly, former general and secretary of Homeland Security.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Chief of Staff Kelly named

events.title: Trump comments on white nationalist rally
events.description: After a rally of white nationalists and neo-Nazis in Charlottesville, Virginia, Trump denounces the violence on “both sides.” He is strongly criticized for not denouncing white supremacy. He later condemns the attack that killed Heather Heyer.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Trump comments on white nationalist rally

events.title: Bannon resigns
events.description: Stephen Bannon resigns from the White House. Bannon was a chief strategist in the White House and campaign aid for President Trump. He helped develop the administration’s nationalist and populist approaches to immigration policy.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Bannon resigns

events.title: US increases presence in Afghanistan
events.description: President Trump announces a new US strategy in Afghanistan, increasing the number of US troops in the country to try to achieve a stable peace.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: US increases presence in Afghanistan

events.title: UN Address
events.description: President Trump addresses the 72nd session of the United Nations General Assembly in New York City. He focuses on his message of "America First" and discusses the ongoing situations with North Korea, Iran, and Syria.
events.impact: Created framework for international cooperation

events.title: Trump officials plead in court
events.description: Trump’s former campaign chairman Paul Manafort pleads not guilty on charges of laundering money. George Papadopoulos, a former foreign policy adviser to the Trump campaign, pleads guilty to lying to the FBI and is cooperating with Mueller investigation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump officials plead in court

events.title: Tour of Asian countries
events.description: President Trump leaves on a five-nation tour of Asia. He travels to Japan, South Korea, China, Vietnam, and the Philippines.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Tour of Asian countries

events.title: Flynn pleads guilty
events.description: President Trump’s former National Security Adviser, Michael Flynn, pleads guilty to lying to the FBI. His guilty plea indicates that Flynn is cooperating with Robert Mueller’s investigation into the Trump administration’s dealings with Russia.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Flynn pleads guilty

events.title: Tax bill signed
events.description: President Trump signs $1.5 trillion tax bill into law. The bill overhauls the federal tax code and is a victory for Republicans. The bill cuts taxes for corporations and the wealthy while delivering only moderate cuts to most Americans.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Tax bill signed

events.title: Government shutdown begins
events.description: The federal government shuts down for three days after Congress cannot reach an agreement to maintain funding for the government.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Government shutdown begins

events.title: Trade War with China Begins
events.description: Prrsident Trump launches a still-running tariff war against China by slapping tariffs of between 30 and 50 per cent on solar panels and washing machines. Tariffs are gradually applied to other US imports from China and to other countries.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trade War with China Begins

events.title: Attorney speaks on affair
events.description: President Trump’s personal lawyer, Michael Cohen, admits that he paid $130,000 of his own money to an adult film actress who had an affair with Trump.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Attorney speaks on affair

events.title: Indictments issued for Russian election interference
events.description: Special Counsel Robert Mueller issues indictments for 13 Russians who allegedly meddled in the 2016 US presidential election.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Indictments issued for Russian election interference

events.title: March for Our Lives
events.description: The March for Our Lives is held in Washington, DC, with companion marches around the country to push for gun control legislation. The march is organized by students from Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School in Parkland, Florida, which had been the site of a mass shooting in February 2018.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: March for Our Lives

events.title: New secretary of state
events.description: Secretary of State Rex Tillerson serves his last day in office. President Trump nominates CIA Director Mike Pompeo to replace him.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: New secretary of state

events.title: Withdrawal from Iran nuclear deal
events.description: President Trump announces that he is pulling the United States out of the deal with Iran to contain its nuclear program.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Withdrawal from Iran nuclear deal

events.title: Pandemic office closed
events.description: Timothy Ziemer, Senior Director for Global Health Security and Biothreats in the National Security Council is dismissed as his position is abolished. Ziemer is in charge of leading a US response against a pandemic. When the coronavirus hits the United States in early 2020, many critics point to abolishing this office as one of the reasons the Trump administration’s response is ineffective.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Pandemic office closed

events.title: US Embassy opens in Jerusalem
events.description: The United States moves its embassy in Israel from Tel Aviv to Jerusalem. The same day, the Israeli army kills more than 50 Palestinians and injures more than 2,000 at the border between Gaza and Israel.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: US Embassy opens in Jerusalem

events.title: Senate confirms new CIA director
events.description: The Senate confirms Trump’s nominee, Gina Haspel, to head the CIA. She is the first woman to be director of the CIA.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Senate confirms new CIA director

events.title: Trump meets with North Korean leader
events.description: President Trump meets with North Korean leader, Kim Jong-un, at a summit in Singapore.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump meets with North Korean leader

events.title: Controversial child separation at the border
events.description: President Trumps signs an executive order ending the policy of separating children from their parents. Between April and June, almost 2,000 children were separated from their parents as they crossed the border into the United States. After public opinion widely condemned the separations, the Trump administration ended the policy.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Controversial child separation at the border

events.title: EPA head resigns
events.description: The head of the Environmental Protection Agency, Scott Pruitt, resigns after being accused of mismanagement and ethics scandals.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: EPA head resigns

events.title: Kavanaugh nominated for SCOTUS
events.description: President Trump nominates Brett Kavanaugh to the US Supreme Court after Justice Anthony Kennedy announces his resignation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Kavanaugh nominated for SCOTUS

events.title: Trump meets with Putin
events.description: President Trump and President Vladimir Putin of Russia meet in Helsinki, Finland, for a summit. During the press conference, Trump refuses to speak out against Russian involvement in the 2016 US elections, and he receives criticism from Democrats and Republicans for not standing up to Russia.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump meets with Putin

events.title: Trumps’s attorney pleads guilty
events.description: President Trump’s former personal lawyer, Michael Cohen, pleads guilty to federal charges, including campaign finance violations and bank and tax fraud. Trump’s former campaign manager, Paul Manafort, is convicted of eight charges including tax evasion and bank fraud.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trumps’s attorney pleads guilty

events.title: Kavanaugh appointed amidst controversy
events.description: Brett Kavanaugh is sworn in as a Supreme Court Justice. His confirmation hearings roiled the nation as three women came forth with accusations of sexual misconduct against Judge Kavanaugh. The Senate Judiciary Committee held a hearing with one of the accusers, Dr. Christine Blasey Ford, and Judge Kavanaugh. The Senate later confirmed his appointment 50 to 48.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Kavanaugh appointed amidst controversy

events.title: Democrats take control of the House
events.description: In the 2018 midterm elections, the Republicans lose the House of Representative but gain seats in the US Senate. Democrats take control of the House, gaining 30 seats and moving the balance of power in the House to 225 Democrats to 197 Republicans. In the Senate, Republicans gained two seats, 53 to 45 (with 2 independent seats).
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Democrats take control of the House

events.title: Attorney General resigns
events.description: The Attorney General of the United States, Jeff Sessions, resigns from office after President Trump requests his resignation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Attorney General resigns

events.title: Trump attorney sentenced
events.description: Michael Cohen, President Trump’s former lawyer, is sentenced to three years in prison after cooperating with federal investigators and Robert Mueller’s investigation and admitting to campaign finance violations when he paid women to remain silent about alleged affairs with Trump before the 2016 presidential election.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump attorney sentenced

events.title: Government shutdown begins
events.description: After failing to reach agreement on a continuing funding resolution, the federal government began a partial shutdown. President Trump and Congress could not agree over funding a border wall between the United States and Mexico.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Government shutdown begins

events.title: Secretary of Defense resigns
events.description: Secretary of Defense James Mattis resigns after President Trump announces he is pulling US troops out of Syria, a decision that Mattis opposes. Trump appoints Patrick Shanahan as acting defense secretary.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Secretary of Defense resigns

events.title: Diversity in Congress
events.description: The 116th Congress is sworn into office. Democrats retake the House, and the new Congress is the most diverse ever with more than 100 women, the first two Native American women representatives, and the first two Muslim women representatives. Nancy Pelosi is sworn in as Speaker of the House.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Diversity in Congress

events.title: Trump announces reopening plan
events.description: After the longest government shutdown in US history, 35 days, President Trump announces an agreement to reopen the government. Although the President had demanded funding for a wall along the southern border, he agrees to reopen the government while negotiations continue over how to fund the building of the wall and border security.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump announces reopening plan

events.title: Budget approval
events.description: Congress passes a budget deal to avert another government shutdown. In the deal, Congress provides $1.375 billion for a border wall but it is far short of the $5.7 billion that President Trump wanted.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Budget approval

events.title: Border wall funding becomes national emergency
events.description: President Trump declares a national emergency to secure funds to build a wall at the southern border.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Border wall funding becomes national emergency

events.title: Mueller report released
events.description: Special Prosecutor Robert Mueller releases his report on the Russian interference in the 2016 presidential election and the Trump campaign’s involvement to the Justice Department.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Mueller report released

events.title: Reelection campaign launched
events.description: At a rally in Florida, President Trump officially launches his 2020 reelection campaign.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Reelection campaign launched

events.title: Trump and Jong-un meet at the DMZ
events.description: Trump shakes hands with Kim at a jointly controlled area inside the DMZ and becomes the first US president to cross over into North Korean territory. Kim then walks over the demarcation line into South Korea with Trump.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump and Jong-un meet at the DMZ

events.title: Trump call to Ukraine president
events.description: President Trump speaks to President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine. In the call, President Trump pushes Zelensky to investigate alleged corruption by Democratic presidential candidate Joe Biden and his son Hunter.
events.impact: Government corruption

events.title: Whistleblower complaint filed
events.description: A member of the US intelligence community files an official complaint against Trump, alleging that the president is trying to get foreign interference to help him win the 2020 election. The complaint is not made public until September.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Whistleblower complaint filed

events.title: Space Command
events.description: President Trump announces the creation of the US Space Command, which will be responsible for military operations in space as part of the Department of Defense.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Space Command

events.title: Democrats begin impeachment inquiries
events.description: Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi announces that the House will begin formal impeachment inquiries against President Trump after a whistleblower complaint against President Trump’s conduct becomes public. At issue is a phone call in which Trump spoke with President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in July 2019 and pressured the Ukrainian president to help Trump with information that might discredit a domestic political rival.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Democrats begin impeachment inquiries

events.title: Impeachment hearings televised
events.description: The House Intelligence Committee begins the public broadcast of the impeachment hearing.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Impeachment hearings televised

events.title: Trump impeached
events.description: The House of Representatives impeaches President Trump on two articles of impeachment: abuse of power (230-197) and obstruction of Congress (229-198). Trump becomes the third US president to be impeached (along with Andrew Johnson in 1868 and Bill Clinton in 1998).
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump impeached

events.title: Soleimani drone strike
events.description: The United States kills General Qasem Soleimani of Iran in a drone attack in Iraq. Iran vows retaliation against the United States.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Soleimani drone strike

events.title: Iran retaliates against the United States
events.description: Iran fires ballistic missiles into two bases in Iraq where US soldiers are stationed. There are no deaths.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Iran retaliates against the United States

events.title: NAFTA replaced
events.description: The Senate ratifies by a vote of 89 to 10, the United States–Mexico–Canada Agreement (USMCA), President Trump's replacement for the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA).
events.impact: Protected rights of people with disabilities

events.title: Limits on travel from China
events.description: The Trump administration limits travel from China into the United States because of the coronavirus outbreak in that country. It prohibits entry into the United States by foreign nationals who have been in mainland China 14 days prior to coming to the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Limits on travel from China

events.title: Senate acquits Trump
events.description: The US Senate votes to acquit President Trump of the articles of impeachment. Senators voted mostly in a party-line vote to acquit the president on the first article of impeachment, Abuse of Power, 52 to 48, and on the second article of impeachment, Obstruction of Congress, 53 to 47.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Senate acquits Trump

events.title: White House Coronavirus Task Force created
events.description: As the coronavirus, which causes Covid-19, spreads worldwide, President Trump appoints Vice President Mike Pence to oversee the management of the virus. Pence works with doctors and health officials to coordinate a response to the virus. By March 1, the virus has killed two people in Washington state and has appeared in a handful of other states, including California, Florida, Illinois, New York, Oregon, and Rhode Island.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: White House Coronavirus Task Force created

events.title: Doha Agreement
events.description: The Trump administration signs a peace agreement, known as the Doha Agreement, with the Taliban to end the U.S. military presence in Afghanistan. The Afghan government is not included in the negotiations. The agreement includes the withdrawal of U.S. and NATO troops from Afghanistan as well as Taliban commitments to not attack U.S. troops and prevent the reestablishment of al Qaeda.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Doha Agreement

events.title: Coronavirus pandemic escalates
events.description: The World Health Organization announces that the coronavirus has become a global pandemic, and President Trump addresses the American public to announce a 30-day travel ban on Europeans coming into the United States. Meanwhile daily life in the United States is disrupted as schools, colleges, and universities move to online classes, events are canceled, and people are encouraged to limit their interactions with others.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Coronavirus pandemic escalates

events.title: Pandemic becomes national emergency
events.description: President Trump declares a national emergency in response to the coronavirus. As Americans are urged to stay home and create social distance with others to slow the virus, Trump announces the national emergency to free up funds and allow government agencies more latitude in protecting people. He also asks every hospital in the country to activate its emergency preparedness plan so that they can meet the needs of Americans everywhere.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Pandemic becomes national emergency

events.title: Economic shutdown and relief efforts
events.description: The United States leads the globe with the most cases of coronavirus. As the virus spreads throughout the country, more states and cities try to limit people’s activities, shutting down business, restaurants, bars, and events. President Trump signs a $2-trillion emergency spending bill, the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security (CARES) Act, which provides money for individuals, corporations and small businesses, and state and local governments.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Economic shutdown and relief efforts

events.title: Unemployment hits 22 million
events.description: A month after much of the US economy shuts down to address the threat of the coronavirus, 22 million Americans file for unemployment. Cases in the country continue to rise as the United States surpasses many other countries in the number of COVID-19 cases.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Unemployment hits 22 million

events.title: Withdrawal from the international arms treaty
events.description: President Donald Trump announces at the National Rifle Association's annual meeting that the United States will drop out of an international arms treaty, which then-President Barack Obama signed in 2013 but the NRA and other conservative groups oppose.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Withdrawal from the international arms treaty

events.title: Spacex launch success
events.description: President Trump attends the second attempted launch of the SpaceX Dragon 2 spacecraft from the Kennedy Space Center. The launch is successful.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Spacex launch success

events.title: Clearing protestors for photo op
events.description: The nation explodes in protests after a Minneapolis police office murders George Floyd, a killing that is captured on film. After a press conference in response to the nationwide and worldwide protests for the death of George Floyd, President Trump walks to the nearby St. John's Church in Lafayette Square for a photo op. In preparation for Trump's arrival, riot police and military police use tear gas and stun grenades to clear protesters assembled at the park.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Clearing protestors for photo op

events.title: Pandemic campaign rally
events.description: President Trump holds an indoor campaign rally in Tulsa, Oklahoma. It is the first public rally he has held since the country largely shut down in March because of COVID-19.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Pandemic campaign rally

events.title: Withdrawal from WHO
events.description: President Trump tells Congress and the United Nations that the United States will withdraw from the World Health Organization, effective 2021.
events.impact: Created framework for international cooperation

events.title: Trump questions mail-in voting
events.description: In one of his tweets, President Trump suggests delaying the November presidential election because of his concerns about mail-in voting. Although only the US Congress can change the date of a presidential election, Trump floats the idea.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump questions mail-in voting

events.title: Social media national security concerns
events.description: President Trump signs executive orders banning the use of TikTok and WeChat in the United States within 45 days if their Chinese parent companies refuse to sell them as a result of national security concerns.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Social media national security concerns

events.title: Trump expands economic relief
events.description: President Trumps signs four executive orders to help the American people during the coronavirus pandemic after negotiations with Democrats in Congress stall. The four orders include a payroll tax holiday, extending unemployment benefits, providing protections to prevent evictions, and suspending student loan payments.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump expands economic relief

events.title: Advocating for education amidst a pandemic
events.description: Cases of COVID-19 continue to rise with the United States, reaching more than 5 million cases and more than 160,000 deaths. Although the Trump administration continues to push for schools to reopen, many schools around the country opt for opening virtually in the fall.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Advocating for education amidst a pandemic

events.title: Bannon arrested
events.description: Steve Bannon, the driving force behind the right-wing Breitbart News website who later became Donald Trump's chief strategist, is arrested and charged with fraud three years after leaving the White House. Bannon is charged with defrauding donors, using money donated to build a wall along the Mexican border for personal expenses.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bannon arrested

events.title: Supreme Court Justice Ginsberg dies
events.description: Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg dies due to complications of metastatic pancreas cancer. President Bill Clinton appointed Ginsburg in 1993. In recent years, RGB served as the most senior member of the court's liberal wing. Her death is less than seven weeks before election day, which opens up a political fight over the future of the court.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Supreme Court Justice Ginsberg dies

events.title: GOP pushes to nominate justice
events.description: Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell reiterates his plans to move forward on President Trump's nominee to fill the vacancy on the US Supreme Court left by the death of Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg. The GOP plan is controversial as McConnell refused to consider President Obama’s nominee after Supreme Court Justice Antonin Scalia died in February 2016, claiming it was too close to the election and the voters should decide. Ginsberg died seven weeks before the 2020 election.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: GOP pushes to nominate justice

events.title: Trump swiftly announces Supreme Court nomination
events.description: President Trump nominates Judge Amy Coney Barret, a conservative Federal Appeals Court Judge, to succeed the late Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg on the US Supreme Court.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump swiftly announces Supreme Court nomination

events.title: NY Times Article on President's Tax Returns
events.description: The New York Times releases an story that claims that President Trump paid no federal income taxes in 10 out of 15 years beginning in 2000.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: NY Times Article on President's Tax Returns

events.title: POTUS and FLOTUS test positive
events.description: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump test positive for coronavirus. By October 5, numerous White House officials and staff, members of the press, and US senators test positive.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: POTUS and FLOTUS test positive

events.title: Commission cancels second debate
events.description: The commison on presiential debates decides to cancel the second debate between Donald Trump and Joe Bden over concerns of the Presients recent Covid-19 diagnosis. The third and final debate takes place on October 22 in Nashville, TN.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Commission cancels second debate

events.title: GOP accuses social media of censorship
events.description: GOP Senate votes to supoena Facebook and Twitter CEO's Marc Zuckerburg and Jack Dorsey to force them to address accusations of censorship in the closing weeks of the presidential campaign. This comes directly after these platforms banned all user's ability to share a NY post article regarding allegations of the Biden family business relations and deals in Ukraine.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: GOP accuses social media of censorship

events.title: GOP confirms SCOTUS
events.description: The Senate votes to confirm President Donald Trump's supreme court nominee Judge Amy Coney, eight days before the general presidential election.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: GOP confirms SCOTUS

events.title: Trump's final campaign rallies
events.description: In the last few days of his campaign before the election, Donald Trump hosts several large campaign rallies in key swing states, gathering large crowds of supporters
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump's final campaign rallies

events.title: Election Day
events.description: On Election Day, President Trump carries 23 states and 213 electoral college votes. The race is close in many states due to unprecedented amounts of mail in ballots, and no winner is declared on election night.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Election Day

events.title: Trump declares victory
events.description: In the early morning hours, President Trump declares victory in the 2020 presidential election and claims the remaining ballots should be cast out due to fraud.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump declares victory

events.title: Press calls race for Biden
events.description: The Associated Press, along with many other news networks, declares former Vice President Joe Biden winner of the presidential election. Biden will become the 46th president of the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Press calls race for Biden

events.title: Trump campaign contests election
events.description: Directly after the election results are announced, President Trump and his campaign began filing lawsuits across key swing states in an attempt to contest the election by claiming mass amounts of voter fraud from mail-in ballots.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump campaign contests election

events.title: Justice Department concludes voter fraud investigation
events.description: US Attorney General William Barr announces that after their detailed investigation, the Justice Departmetn found no widespread voter fraud.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Justice Department concludes voter fraud investigation

events.title: FDA approves COVID-19 vaccine
events.description: The US Food and Drug Administration issues the first emergency use authorization for a Coronavirus vaccine in individuals 16 and older. The authorization allows the Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine to be distributed.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: FDA approves COVID-19 vaccine

events.title: Supreme Court rejects claims of voter fraud
events.description: The Supreme Court rejects the case brought by the Texas justice department and 17 states that alleged widespread voter fraud to contest the results of the election.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Supreme Court rejects claims of voter fraud

events.title: Bill Barr Resigns
events.description: US Attorney General William Barr announces his resignation and that he will step down before the end of the Trump presidency. Barr's resignation comes shortly after Trump discusses firing the attorney general after his announcement that the Justice Department has not found evidence of voter fraud.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bill Barr Resigns

events.title: Electoral College declares the 46th POTUS
events.description: The Electoral College officially votes Joe Biden as president and Senator Kamala Harris as vice president of the United States. This decision ends the legal battle initiated by the Trump campaign which alleged mass amounts of voter fraud in the election.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Electoral College declares the 46th POTUS

events.title: Phone call leaked
events.description: President Trump calls the Secretary of State of Georgia to pressure him to "find" votes so that Trump can win Georgia. The Secretary tapes the conversation and leaks it to the press the next day.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Phone call leaked

events.title: Mob attacks US Capitol
events.description: Trump supporters and white supremacists gather to protest the results of the 2020 presidential election. A mob overwhelms Capitol police and pushes their way into the US Capitol, where Congress is meeting to certify the election results. As members of Congress rush out of harm's way, police are unable to contain the intruders who broke windows, destroyed property, and trespassed through the building. The police shoot and kill one pro-Trump demonstrator as she tries to break through a door, and one Capitol police officer dies of injuries sustained during the riot.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Mob attacks US Capitol

events.title: Second impeachment
events.description: Donald Trump becomes the first US president to be impeached twice, when the US House of Representatives impeaches him for “incitement of insurrection,” for the attack on the Capitol on January 6. The House votes 232 to 197 with 10 Republicans supporting the impeachment.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Second impeachment

events.title: Trump leaves office
events.description: Donald Trump leaves the White House on the morning of January 20. He is the first president since 1869 to skip his successor's inauguration. He and his family fly to Florida.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump leaves office

events.title: Biden becomes president
events.description: Joe Biden is inaugurated as the 46th president of the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Biden becomes president

events.title: Flurry of executive orders
events.description: In his first few days in office, President Joe Biden signs numerous executive orders, including rejoining the World Health Organization and the Paris Climate Accord, allowing transgendered people to serve in the military, and creating the Covid-19 Health Equity Task Force.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Flurry of executive orders

events.title: Impeachment trial begins
events.description: The US Senate begins hearing arguments in the second impeachment trial of Donald Trump.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Impeachment trial begins

events.title: Senate acquits Trump
events.description: The US Senate acquits former President Donald Trump in his second impeachment trial with a vote of 57 to 43.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Senate acquits Trump

events.title: Third Covid-19 vaccine
events.description: FDA approves a third vaccine by Johnson and Johnson for Covid-19.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Third Covid-19 vaccine

events.title: American Rescue Plan
events.description: On the anniversary of the Covid-19 shutdown, President Biden signs the American Rescue Plan Act, a $1.9-trillion economic stimulus bill to combat the effects of the Covid-19 pandemic.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: American Rescue Plan

events.title: 100 million vaccinations
events.description: The country reaches 100 million vaccinations for COVID-19, exceeding the Biden administration's goal of administering 100 million shots in the first 100 days.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: 100 million vaccinations

events.title: The American Jobs Plan
events.description: In a speech in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, President Biden announces the details of the American Jobs Plan, which will modernize the country's infrastructure, combat the effects of climate change, and help middle-class workers.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: The American Jobs Plan

events.title: Addresses Congress
events.description: In honor of his first 100 days in office, President Biden gives a speech to Congress. It focuses on his administration's accomplishments so far and outlines the president's legislative priorities.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Addresses Congress

events.title: January 6 commission blocked
events.description: The US Senate Republicans block a bill to create an independent commission on the January 6 Capitol riot.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: January 6 commission blocked

events.title: Biden ends bipartisan talks
events.description: After weeks of negotiations, Biden ends bipartisan talks on an infrastructure bill after the administration and Republicans remained far apart on its details.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Biden ends bipartisan talks

events.title: First trip to Europe
events.description: President Biden leaves on his first trip to Europe, which includes a meeting of the G7 and a NATO summit.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: First trip to Europe

events.title: Juneteenth holiday
events.description: Joe Biden signs a bill creating a federal holiday for Juneteenth, June 19th, to commemorate the end of slavery in the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Juneteenth holiday

events.title: Drawdown of US forces in Afghanistan
events.description: President Biden gives an update on the drawdown of US troops from Afghanistan, due to be completed in September.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Drawdown of US forces in Afghanistan

events.title: Taliban take over Afghanistan
events.description: As the United States draws down the US troop presence in Afghanistan, the Taliban seize Kabul and take over the Afghan government.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Taliban take over Afghanistan

events.title: Terror attacks at Afghanistan airport
events.description: Thirteen US military service members and dozens of Afghan civilians are killed at the airport in Kabul, Afghanistan, after a suicide bomber sets off explosives in a crowd of people. The ISIS-K terrorist group claims responsibility.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Terror attacks at Afghanistan airport

events.title: War in Afghanistan ends
events.description: The last US troops leave Afghanistan after a 20-year war.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: War in Afghanistan ends

events.title: Off-year elections
events.description: In off-year elections, Republican Glenn Youngkin won the Virginia governor's race, while Democrat Phil Murphy won reelection as governor in New Jersey.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Off-year elections

events.title: Biden signs infrastructure bill
events.description: President Biden signs the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, a $1.2 trillion bill to improve roads, bridges, public transit, as well as increased access to clean water and the internet.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Biden signs infrastructure bill

events.title: Omicron variant emerges
events.description: President Biden gives a speech about a new variant of the coronavirus known as the Omicron variant and the federal government's plan to send millions of at-home tests to Americans.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Omicron variant emerges

events.title: Anniversary of January 6
events.description: On the one year anniversary of the January 6 Capitol riot, President Biden addresses the American people from the US Capitol.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Anniversary of January 6

events.title: Sanctions against Russia
events.description: After President Vladimir Putin of Russia sends troops into two separatist-held regions of Ukraine, President Biden announces US sanctions against Russia. The sanctions target Russian finances and banking.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Sanctions against Russia

events.title: Russian invasion of Ukraine
events.description: Russia invades Ukraine.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Russian invasion of Ukraine

events.title: Supreme Court nomination
events.description: President Biden nominates Judge Ketanji Brown Jackson to the US Supreme Court.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Supreme Court nomination

events.title: Ban on Russian oil and gas
events.description: President Biden announces that the United States will ban imports of natural gas and oil from Russia in an attempt to put pressure on President Putin to stop the war in Ukraine.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Ban on Russian oil and gas

events.title: Travel to Europe
events.description: President Biden travels to Europe in support of Ukraine. He attends an EU Summit meeting and a NATO meeting and gives a speech in Poland.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Travel to Europe

events.title: Antilynching legislation
events.description: President Biden signs the Emmett Till Antilynching Act, making lynching a federal crime.
events.impact: Racial terrorism and murder

events.title: Supreme Court confirmation
events.description: The US Senate confirms Judge Ketanji Brown Jackson as the first African American woman to the US Supreme Court by a vote of 53 to 47.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Supreme Court confirmation

events.title: School shooting in Texas
events.description: In Uvalde, Texas, a gunman open fires at an elementary school, killing 19 children and 2 teachers. It is the deadliest school shooting in almost a decade.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: School shooting in Texas

events.title: Proposed gun control regulations
events.description: President Biden gives a speech, calling on Congress to pass a ban on assault rifles as well as other gun control measures.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Proposed gun control regulations

events.title: Supreme Court overturns federal right to abortion
events.description: In Dobbs v Jackson , the US Supreme Court rules that the Constitution does not provide the right to an abortion. The decision overrules both Roe v. Wade (1973) and Planned Parenthood v. Casey (1992).
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Supreme Court overturns federal right to abortion

events.title: Safer Communities Act
events.description: President Biden signs the Safer Communities Act, which addresses issues related to gun safety, school safety, and mental health to try to reduce gun violence. This bill is the first major federal gun control law in decades.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Safer Communities Act

events.title: Rising inflation
events.description: Inflation continues to rise, with the Consumer Price Index climbing 9.1 percent from a year earlier in June 2022.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Rising inflation

events.title: Al Qaeda leader killed
events.description: The United States launches a drone strike in Afghanistan that kills Al Qaeda leader, Ayman al-Zawahiri.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Al Qaeda leader killed

events.title: CHIPS and Science Act
events.description: On August 9, 2022, President Joe Biden signed the CHIPS and Science Act, which appropriated $280 billion for semiconductor and chip research and manufacturing.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: CHIPS and Science Act

events.title: Inflation Reduction Act
events.description: President Biden signs the Inflation Reduction Act, which lowers health care costs and focuses on tackling the climate crisis by reducing pollution and investing in clean energy.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Inflation Reduction Act

events.title: Student loan forgiveness
events.description: The Biden administration announces that it will cancel up to $10,000 in student debt for most borrowers, up to $20,000 for Pell Grant recipients. The US Supreme Court strikes down the program in June 2023.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Student loan forgiveness

events.title: Battle for the soul of the nation
events.description: In Independence Hall in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, President Biden gives a speech ahead of the midterm elections that focuses on the state of American democracy.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Battle for the soul of the nation

events.title: Marijuana pardons
events.description: President Biden pardons all people convicted of marijuana possession under federal law and asks for a review of whether marijuana should be in the same legal category as heroin and LSD.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Marijuana pardons

events.title: Midterm elections
events.description: Although Republicans retook control of the House, Democrats performed better than expected in midterm elections.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Midterm elections

events.title: Special counsel for Trump investigations
events.description: Attorney General Merrick Garland announces that the Justice Department will appoint a special counsel to investigate former President Trump in the January 6 attack and the handling of sensitive government documents.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Special counsel for Trump investigations

events.title: Same-Sex Marriage Bill
events.description: Biden signs the Respect for Marriage Act into law. The bill provides federal protections for same-sex and interracial marriages.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Same-Sex Marriage Bill

events.title: Electoral reform
events.description: President Biden signs the Electoral Count Reform and Presidential Transition Improvement Act of 2022, election reforms designed to prevent another January 6 attack. Along with other provisions, it outlines processes for how to handle disputes over counting electoral votes and the role of the vice president.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Electoral reform

events.title: Special Counsel appointed
events.description: After classified government documents are found in Biden's home, Attorney General Merrick Garland appoints Robert Hur as a special counsel to investigate the matter.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Special Counsel appointed

events.title: US tanks to Urkraine
events.description: President Biden announces that the United States will send 31 battle tanks to Ukraine to help them defeat the Russian invasion.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: US tanks to Urkraine

events.title: Chinese balloon shot down
events.description: President Biden orders military aircraft to shoot down a high-altitude balloon from China that has flown over the country.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Chinese balloon shot down

events.title: Visit to Ukraine
events.description: After a secret journey, President Biden arrives in Kyiv, Ukraine, to meet with President Volodymyr Zelensky. It is the first time a modern-day president ventured into a war zone that was not held by American forces.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Visit to Ukraine

events.title: First veto
events.description: President Biden issues the first veto of his presidency. He vetoes a bill that would ban the government from considering environmental impacts when making investments for retirement plans.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: First veto

events.title: Reelection campaign
events.description: President Biden announces his campaign for reelection for a second term as president.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Reelection campaign

events.title: Debt ceiling deal reached
events.description: President Biden and Speaker of the House Kevin McCarthy reached a deal to raise the nation's debt ceiling and avoid an historic government default.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Debt ceiling deal reached

events.title: US Supreme Court ends affirmative action
events.description: The US Supreme Court rules that colleges and universities cannot used race-based affirmative action in admissions.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: US Supreme Court ends affirmative action

events.title: Trip to Maui after wildfire devestation
events.description: President and First Lady Biden travel to Maui, Hawaii, to tour the devestation from a wildfire on the island. A few hundred people died after the fires spread through the town of Lahaina.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trip to Maui after wildfire devestation

events.title: Office of Gun Violence Prevention
events.description: President Biden announces the creation of the White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Office of Gun Violence Prevention

events.title: Biden visits striking workers
events.description: President Biden visits members of the United Auto Workers union who are striking to show his support for organized labor. It is the first time a modern president has joined a picket line.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Biden visits striking workers

events.title: Speaker of House ousted
events.description: Far-right Republicans oust Speaker Kevin McCarthy (R-Calif) because of ideological differences. It is the first time in US history that members of the House remove the Speaker of the House in the middle of his term.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Speaker of House ousted

events.title: Israel declares war
events.description: Hamas, a militant Palestinian organization that runs the government in the Gaza Strip, launches a surprise attack into Israeli territory and settlements near the Gaza border, killing more than 1,200 people and seizing some 250 hostages. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel declares war against Hamas.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Israel declares war

events.title: Biden travels to Israel
events.description: President Biden travels to Israel to signal US support for the Israelis after the Hamas attack.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Biden travels to Israel

events.title: Speaker elected
events.description: Representative Mike Johnson of Lousiana is elected Speaker of the House.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Speaker elected

events.title: Executive order on artificial intelligence
events.description: President Biden signs an executive order on artificial intelligence that attempts to protect Americans from the risks of AI.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Executive order on artificial intelligence

events.title: APEC Summit meeting
events.description: President Biden attends the APEC Summit meeting in San Francisco, California. The day before the meeting begins, he meets with President Xi Jinping of China.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: APEC Summit meeting

events.title: President's birthday
events.description: President Biden celebrates his 81st birthday, making him the oldest US president in American history.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: President's birthday

events.title: Funeral for First Lady Rosalynn Carter
events.description: President and First Lady Biden attend the funeral of Rosalynn Carter, wife of President Jimmy Carter, in Georgia.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Funeral for First Lady Rosalynn Carter

events.title: Meeting with President of Ukraine
events.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine meets with President Biden at the White House.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Meeting with President of Ukraine

events.title: Campaign begins
events.description: The day before the third anniversary of the January 6th attack on the US Capitol, President Biden gives a campaign speech in Pennsylvania, laying out his case for reelection.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Campaign begins

events.title: US strikes in Middle East
events.description: After drone attacks in Jordan kill three US soldiers, the United States carries out aerial strikes in Syria, Yemen, and Iraq against Iran-backed militias.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: US strikes in Middle East

events.title: Justice Department declines to prosecute
events.description: Special counsel from the Department of Justice releases a report and declines to prosecute President Biden for mishandling classified documents after his vice presidency, but the report personally attacks the President, calling him "an elderly man with poor memory."
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Justice Department declines to prosecute

events.title: Aid to Ukraine, Israel, and Taiwan
events.description: President Joe Biden signs a $95.3 billion package of aid to Ukraine, Israel, and Taiwan, after months of debate in Congress led to a delay in approving the package.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Aid to Ukraine, Israel, and Taiwan

events.title: Executive order to limit migrants
events.description: President Biden issues an executive order limiting asylum seekers at the US border. The order blocks migrants from crossing into the United States seeking asylum if the number of asylum seekers is more than 2,500 per day.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Executive order to limit migrants

events.title: Hunter Biden found guilty
events.description: President Biden's son Hunter is found guilty on three counts of lying on a federal application for a firearm.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Hunter Biden found guilty

events.title: Disasterous debate
events.description: In the first debate of the 2024 presidential election, President Biden turns in a disasterous performance that leaves many questioning his ability to campaign for reelection.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Disasterous debate

events.title: Assassination attempt on Trump
events.description: At a rally in Pennsylvania, a gunman attempts to assassinate former President Donald Trump. He is grazed on his ear, but survives with no serious injuries. A spectator at the rally is killed, and two people are seriously injured.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Assassination attempt on Trump

events.title: Withdrawing from the campaign
events.description: In a letter posted on social media, President Biden announces that he will not seek reelection and will withdraw from the 2024 presidential campaign. He endorses Vice President Kamala Harris.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Withdrawing from the campaign

events.title: Prisoner swap
events.description: President Biden announces a prisoner swap with Russia, resulting in the return of 16 people in exchange for 8 Russians.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Prisoner swap

events.title: Lower drug prices
events.description: President Biden and Vice President Harris announce an agreement with major pharmaceutical companies to lower prices on widely used prescription drugs.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Lower drug prices

events.title: Democratic National Convention
events.description: President Biden speaks at the Democratic National Convention in Chicago, Illinois. Two days later on August 21, Vice President Kamala Harris accepts the Democratic nomination for president.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Democratic National Convention

events.title: Election Day
events.description: Former President Donald Trump wins the 2024 presidential election, defeating Vice President Kamala Harris. Republicans also gained control of the US House and Senate.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Election Day

events.title: Trump visits White House
events.description: President Biden hosts President-elect Trump at the White House to discuss the transition of power.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump visits White House

events.title: Trip to Amazon
events.description: President Biden becomes the first sitting US president to visit the Amazon rainforest in Brazil.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trip to Amazon

events.title: Pardons son
events.description: President Biden pardons his son, Hunter Biden, from federal convictions related to tax evasion and illegally purchasing a gun.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Pardons son

events.title: Fall of Syria
events.description: The government of Bashar al-Assad of Syria falls as rebel groups take over the government. Assad flees to Russia, ending the Syrian civil war that began in 2011.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Fall of Syria

events.title: Preemptive pardons
events.description: On his last day in office, President Joe Biden issues preemptive pardons to Dr. Anthony Fauci, General Mark Milley, and members of the January 6 congressional committee to protect them from possible prosecution by the incoming Trump administration.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Preemptive pardons
